Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\Admin\.claude\skills\subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Why subagents:** You delegate tasks to specialized agents with isolated context. By precisely crafting their instructions and context, you ensure they stay focused and succeed at their task. They should never inherit your session's context or history — you construct exactly what they need. This also preserves your own context for coordination work.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

**Continuous execution:** Do not pause to check in with your human partner between tasks. Execute all tasks from the plan without stopping. The only reasons to stop are: BLOCKED status you cannot resolve, ambiguity that genuinely prevents progress, or all tasks complete. "Should I continue?" prompts and progress summaries waste their time — they asked you to execute the plan, so execute it.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Model Selection

Use the least powerful model that can handle each role to conserve cost and increase speed.

**Mechanical implementation tasks** (isolated functions, clear specs, 1-2 files): use a fast, cheap model. Most implementation tasks are mechanical when the plan is well-specified.

**Integration and judgment tasks** (multi-file coordination, pattern matching, debugging): use a standard model.

**Architecture, design, and review tasks**: use the most capable available model.

**Task complexity signals:**
- Touches 1-2 files with a complete spec → cheap model
- Touches multiple files with integration concerns → standard model
- Requires design judgment or broad codebase understanding → most capable model

## Handling Implementer Status

Implementer subagents report one of four statuses. Handle each appropriately:

**DONE:** Proceed to spec compliance review.

**DONE_WITH_CONCERNS:** The implementer completed the work but flagged doubts. Read the concerns before proceeding. If the concerns are about correctness or scope, address them before review. If they're observations (e.g., "this file is getting large"), note them and proceed to review.

**NEEDS_CONTEXT:** The implementer needs information that wasn't provided. Provide the missing context and re-dispatch.

**BLOCKED:** The implementer cannot complete the task. Assess the blocker:
1. If it's a context problem, provide more context and re-dispatch with the same model
2. If the task requires more reasoning, re-dispatch with a more capable model
3. If the task is too large, break it into smaller pieces
4. If the plan itself is wrong, escalate to the human

**Never** ignore an escalation or force the same model to retry without changes. If the implementer said it's stuck, something needs to change.

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/superpowers/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - Ensures isolated workspace (creates one or verifies existing)
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

<cell id="cell-0"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># DigiSteel-YOLO v2: Training & Evaluation Pipeline

## WFCA + EMA + GhostConv + Inner-WIoU

**Team:** Hazem Elerefy, Youssef Sherif, Mohamed Salah, Moamen Esmat, Mahmoud Hisham  
**Supervisor:** Dr. Tarek Ghoneimy  
**Deadline:** Sunday June 7, 2026

---

### Pipeline:
1. Setup environment + clone repo
2. Register custom modules (WFCA, EMA, GhostConv)
3. Download NEU-DET dataset
4. Train baseline YOLOv11n (100 epochs)
5. Train DigiSteel v2 (100 epochs)
6. Evaluate both models (clean + robustness)
7. Comparison table

**⚠️ Enable GPU:** Runtime → Change runtime type → **T4 GPU**</cell id="cell-0">
<cell id="cell-1"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>## 1. Setup Environment</cell id="cell-1">
<cell id="cell-2">#@title 1.1 Install Dependencies

<cell id="cell-4"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>## 2. Register Custom Modules + Verify</cell id="cell-4">
<cell id="cell-5">#@title 2.1 Register WFCA, EMA, GhostConv with Ultralytics
from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules
from digisteel.modules import WFCA, EMA, GhostConv, InnerWIoULoss, inner_wiou_iou
from digisteel.perturbations import PerturbationSuite

# Register custom modules so Ultralytics can parse digisteel_v2.yaml
register_custom_modules()

print('✓ Custom modules registered with Ultralytics')

print(f'  EMA: {EMA}')
print(f'  GhostConv: {GhostConv}')

<cell id="cell-10">#@title 3.3 Prepare Dataset in YOLO Format
import json
import random
import shutil
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path

NEU_DET_CLASSES = {
    'crazing': 0, 'inclusion': 1, 'patches': 2,
    'pitted_surface': 3, 'rolled-in_scale': 4, 'scratches': 5
}

def convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path, img_w=200, img_h=200):
    tree = ET.parse(xml_path)
    root = tree.getroot()
    lines = []
    for obj in root.findall('object'):
        name = obj.find('name').text
        if name not in NEU_DET_CLASSES:
            continue
        cid = NEU_DET_CLASSES[name]
        bb = obj.find('bndbox')
        xmin, ymin = float(bb.find('xmin').text), float(bb.find('ymin').text)
        xmax, ymax = float(bb.find('xmax').text), float(bb.find('ymax').text)
        cx = ((xmin + xmax) / 2) / img_w
        cy = ((ymin + ymax) / 2) / img_h
        w = (xmax - xmin) / img_w
        h = (ymax - ymin) / img_h
        lines.append(f'{cid} {cx:.6f} {cy:.6f} {w:.6f} {h:.6f}')
    return '\n'.join(lines)

# Check if YOLO format already exists
yolo_dir = neu_dir / 'yolo'
if yolo_dir.exists() and len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / 'train').glob('*.jpg'))) > 100:
    print('✓ YOLO format dataset already exists')
    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:
        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))
        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')
else:
    print('Converting to YOLO format...')
    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:
        (yolo_dir / 'images' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Find source images and annotations
    train_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'train_images').glob('*.jpg'))
    valid_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'valid_images').glob('*.jpg'))
    all_imgs = train_imgs + valid_imgs
    ann_dirs = [neu_dir / 'train_annotations'] * len(train_imgs) + [neu_dir / 'valid_annotations'] * len(valid_imgs)

    converted = 0
    for img_path, ann_dir in zip(all_imgs, ann_dirs):
        xml_path = ann_dir / (img_path.stem + '.xml')
        if not xml_path.exists():
            continue
        yolo_text = convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path)

        random.seed(42 + hash(img_path.name) % 10000)
        r = random.random()
        split = 'train' if r < 0.7 else ('val' if r < 0.9 else 'test')

        shutil.copy2(img_path, yolo_dir / 'images' / split / img_path.name)
        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split / (img_path.stem + '.txt')).write_text(yolo_text)
        converted += 1

    print(f'✓ Converted {converted} images')
    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:
        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))
        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')</cell id="cell-10">

<cell id="cell-17"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>## 6. Robustness Evaluation</cell id="cell-17">
<cell id="cell-18">#@title 6.1 Run Robustness Sweep (6 types x 4 levels = 24 points)
import cv2
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from ultralytics import YOLO
from digisteel.perturbations import PerturbationSuite
from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules

register_custom_modules()

def evaluate_robust(model_path, data_yaml, imgsz=640):
    """Evaluate model on clean + 24 perturbation points."""
    model = YOLO(model_path)
    suite = PerturbationSuite()

    # Clean evaluation
    clean_val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)
    clean_map = clean_val.box.map50

    results = [{'perturbation': 'clean', 'level': 0, 'mAP50': clean_map}]

    # Perturbed evaluation
    # For each perturbation, we apply it to the val images temporarily
    # and run inference
    for config in suite.all_configs():
        # Apply perturbation to dataset images
        val_img_dir = Path('datasets/NEU-DET/yolo/images/val')
        backup_dir = Path('/tmp/val_backup')
        perturbed_dir = Path('/tmp/val_perturbed')

        # Backup originals once
        if not backup_dir.exists():
            backup_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
            for img in val_img_dir.glob('*.jpg'):
                import shutil
                shutil.copy2(img, backup_dir / img.name)

        # Apply perturbation
        for img in backup_dir.glob('*.jpg'):
            orig = cv2.imread(str(img))
            degraded = suite.apply(orig, config.name, config.level)
            cv2.imwrite(str(val_img_dir / img.name), degraded)

        # Evaluate on perturbed images
        val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)
        results.append({
            'perturbation': config.name,
            'level': config.level,
            'mAP50': val.box.map50
        })

    # Restore originals
    for img in backup_dir.glob('*.jpg'):
        import shutil
        shutil.copy2(img, val_img_dir / img.name)

    return pd.DataFrame(results)

# Run on both models
baseline_path = f'runs/baseline_neu_det_seed{SEED}/weights/best.pt'
v2_path = f'runs/digisteel_v2_neu_det_seed{SEED}/weights/best.pt'

if Path(baseline_path).exists():
    print('Evaluating baseline robustness...')
    baseline_robust = evaluate_robust(baseline_path, config_path)
    baseline_robust.to_csv('evals/baseline_robustness.csv', index=False)
    print(f'✓ Baseline robustness saved')
else:
    print(f'⚠ Baseline not found: {baseline_path}')
    baseline_robust = None

if Path(v2_path).exists():
    print('Evaluating DigiSteel v2 robustness...')
    v2_robust = evaluate_robust(v2_path, config_path)
    v2_robust.to_csv('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv', index=False)
    print(f'✓ DigiSteel v2 robustness saved')
else:
    print(f'⚠ DigiSteel v2 not found: {v2_path}')
    v2_robust = None</cell id="cell-18">
<cell id="cell-19"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>## 7. Comparison Table</cell id="cell-19">
<cell id="cell-20">#@title 7.1 Baseline vs DigiSteel v2 Comparison
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

print('='*80)
print('  DigiSteel v2 vs Baseline — Final Comparison')
print('='*80)

# Load robustness results if available
baseline_csv = Path('evals/baseline_robustness.csv')
v2_csv = Path('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv')

if baseline_csv.exists() and v2_csv.exists():
    b = pd.read_csv(baseline_csv)
    v = pd.read_csv(v2_csv)

    clean_b = b[b['perturbation'] == 'clean']['mAP50'].values[0]
    clean_v = v[v['perturbation'] == 'clean']['mAP50'].values[0]

    avg_b = b[b['perturbation'] != 'clean']['mAP50'].mean()
    avg_v = v[v['perturbation'] != 'clean']['mAP50'].mean()

    stability_b = avg_b / clean_b if clean_b > 0 else 0
    stability_v = avg_v / clean_v if clean_v > 0 else 0

    print(f'\n{'Metric':<30} {'Baseline':>12} {'DigiSteel v2':>12} {'Delta':>10}')
    print('-'*70)
    print(f"{'Clean mAP@0.5':<30} {clean_b:>12.4f} {clean_v:>12.4f} {clean_v-clean_b:>+10.4f}")
    print(f"{'Avg Perturbed mAP@0.5':<30} {avg_b:>12.4f} {avg_v:>12.4f} {avg_v-avg_b:>+10.4f}")
    print(f"{'Stability Ratio':<30} {stability_b:>12.4f} {stability_v:>12.4f} {stability_v-stability_b:>+10.4f}")

    # Per-perturbation breakdown
    print(f'\n{'Perturbation':<25} {'Level':>6} {'Baseline':>10} {'v2':>10} {'Delta':>10}')
    print('-'*65)
    for _, brow in b[b['perturbation'] != 'clean'].iterrows():
        vrow = v[(v['perturbation'] == brow['perturbation']) & (v['level'] == brow['level'])]
        if len(vrow) > 0:
            vmap = vrow['mAP50'].values[0]
            delta = vmap - brow['mAP50']
            print(f"{brow['perturbation']:<25} {int(brow['level']):>6} {brow['mAP50']:>10.4f} {vmap:>10.4f} {delta:>+10.4f}")

else:
    print('⚠ Robustness CSVs not found. Run the robustness evaluation cell first.')
    print('  Expected: evals/baseline_robustness.csv, evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv')</cell id="cell-20">

<cell id="cell-24"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>---

## Notes

- **If session times out:** Re-run cells 1-2 (setup), then skip to the training cell that was interrupted
- **100 epochs ≈ 1-2 hours** on T4 GPU per model
- **Total time ≈ 3-4 hours** for both models + evaluation
- **Inner-WIoU:** The current notebook uses standard Ultralytics loss. For full Inner-WIoU integration, use `scripts/train.py` which patches BboxLoss via `DigiSteelTrainer`
- **Custom module registration** must happen before loading the v2 YAML — always run cell 2.1 first</cell id="cell-24">

cells.source:
# DigiSteel-YOLO v2: Training & Evaluation Pipeline

## WFCA + EMA + GhostConv + Inner-WIoU

**Team:** Hazem Elerefy, Youssef Sherif, Mohamed Salah, Moamen Esmat, Mahmoud Hisham  
**Supervisor:** Dr. Tarek Ghoneimy  
**Deadline:** Sunday June 7, 2026

---

### Pipeline:
1. Setup environment + clone repo
2. Register custom modules (WFCA, EMA, GhostConv)
3. Download NEU-DET dataset
4. Train baseline YOLOv11n (100 epochs)
5. Train DigiSteel v2 (100 epochs)
6. Evaluate both models (clean + robustness)
7. Comparison table

**⚠️ Enable GPU:** Runtime → Change runtime type → **T4 GPU**
## 1. Setup Environment

#@title 1.1 Install Dependencies
!pip install ultralytics>=8.3.0,<8.4.0 -q
!pip install opencv-python albumentations numpy pandas matplotlib seaborn tqdm pyyaml -q

import torch
import ultralytics
print(f'PyTorch: {torch.__version__}')
print(f'CUDA: {torch.cuda.is_available()}')

    print(f'GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}')
    print(f'Memory: {torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).total_mem / 1024**3:.1f} GB')
print(f'Ultralytics: {ultralytics.__version__}')

#@title 1.2 Clone Repository
import os
os.chdir('/content')

REPO_URL = 'https://github.com/hazemelerefey/DigiSteel-YOLO.git'  #@param {type:"string"}
BRANCH = 'main'  #@param {type:"string"}

if not os.path.exists('DigiSteel-YOLO'):
    !git clone -b $BRANCH $REPO_URL
else:
    os.chdir('DigiSteel-YOLO')
    !git pull origin $BRANCH
    os.chdir('/content')

os.chdir('DigiSteel-YOLO')
!pip install -e . -q

from pathlib import Path
for d in ['datasets', 'runs', 'evals', 'weights']:
    Path(d).mkdir(exist_ok=True)

print(f'Project dir: {os.getcwd()}')
## 2. Register Custom Modules + Verify

#@title 2.1 Register WFCA, EMA, GhostConv with Ultralytics
from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules
from digisteel.modules import WFCA, EMA, GhostConv, InnerWIoULoss, inner_wiou_iou
from digisteel.perturbations import PerturbationSuite

# Register custom modules so Ultralytics can parse digisteel_v2.yaml
register_custom_modules()

print('✓ Custom modules registered with Ultralytics')
print(f'  WFCA: {WFCA}')
print(f'  EMA: {EMA}')
print(f'  GhostConv: {GhostConv}')
print(f'  InnerWIoULoss: {InnerWIoULoss}')
print(f'  PerturbationSuite: {PerturbationSuite}')

#@title 2.2 Quick Smoke Test — Build DigiSteel v2 Model
from ultralytics import YOLO

# This will parse configs/models/digisteel_v2.yaml and build the model
model_v2 = YOLO('configs/models/digisteel_v2.yaml')

# Test forward pass
import torch
dummy = torch.randn(1, 3, 640, 640).to('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
model_v2.model.to(dummy.device)
with torch.no_grad():
    out = model_v2.model(dummy)

total_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model_v2.model.parameters())
print(f'✓ DigiSteel v2 model built successfully')
print(f'  Parameters: {total_params:,} ({total_params/1e6:.2f}M)')
print(f'  Output shape: {out[0].shape if isinstance(out, tuple) else out.shape}')
## 3. Download NEU-DET Dataset

#@title 3.1 Configure Kaggle API
from pathlib import Path
import os

kaggle_dir = Path.home() / '.kaggle'
kaggle_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

KAGGLE_USERNAME = 'hazemelerefy'  #@param {type:"string"}
KAGGLE_KEY = ''  #@param {type:"string"}

else:

#@title 3.3 Prepare Dataset in YOLO Format
import json
import random
import shutil
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path

NEU_DET_CLASSES = {
    'crazing': 0, 'inclusion': 1, 'patches': 2,
    'pitted_surface': 3, 'rolled-in_scale': 4, 'scratches': 5
}

def convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path, img_w=200, img_h=200):
    tree = ET.parse(xml_path)
    root = tree.getroot()
    lines = []
    for obj in root.findall('object'):
        name = obj.find('name').text
        if name not in NEU_DET_CLASSES:
            continue
        cid = NEU_DET_CLASSES[name]
        bb = obj.find('bndbox')
        xmin, ymin = float(bb.find('xmin').text), float(bb.find('ymin').text)
        xmax, ymax = float(bb.find('xmax').text), float(bb.find('ymax').text)
        cx = ((xmin + xmax) / 2) / img_w
        cy = ((ymin + ymax) / 2) / img_h
        w = (xmax - xmin) / img_w
        h = (ymax - ymin) / img_h
        lines.append(f'{cid} {cx:.6f} {cy:.6f} {w:.6f} {h:.6f}')
    return '\n'.join(lines)

# Check if YOLO format already exists
yolo_dir = neu_dir / 'yolo'
if yolo_dir.exists() and len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / 'train').glob('*.jpg'))) > 100:
    print('✓ YOLO format dataset already exists')
    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:
        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))
        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')
else:
    print('Converting to YOLO format...')
    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:
        (yolo_dir / 'images' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Find source images and annotations
    train_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'train_images').glob('*.jpg'))
    valid_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'valid_images').glob('*.jpg'))
    all_imgs = train_imgs + valid_imgs
    ann_dirs = [neu_dir / 'train_annotations'] * len(train_imgs) + [neu_dir / 'valid_annotations'] * len(valid_imgs)

    converted = 0
    for img_path, ann_dir in zip(all_imgs, ann_dirs):
        xml_path = ann_dir / (img_path.stem + '.xml')
        if not xml_path.exists():
            continue
        yolo_text = convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path)

        random.seed(42 + hash(img_path.name) % 10000)
        r = random.random()
        split = 'train' if r < 0.7 else ('val' if r < 0.9 else 'test')

        shutil.copy2(img_path, yolo_dir / 'images' / split / img_path.name)
        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split / (img_path.stem + '.txt')).write_text(yolo_text)
        converted += 1

    print(f'✓ Converted {converted} images')
    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:
        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))
        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')

## 6. Robustness Evaluation

#@title 6.1 Run Robustness Sweep (6 types x 4 levels = 24 points)
import cv2
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from ultralytics import YOLO
from digisteel.perturbations import PerturbationSuite
from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules

register_custom_modules()

def evaluate_robust(model_path, data_yaml, imgsz=640):
    """Evaluate model on clean + 24 perturbation points."""
    model = YOLO(model_path)
    suite = PerturbationSuite()

    # Clean evaluation
    clean_val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)
    clean_map = clean_val.box.map50

    results = [{'perturbation': 'clean', 'level': 0, 'mAP50': clean_map}]

    # Perturbed evaluation
    # For each perturbation, we apply it to the val images temporarily
    # and run inference
    for config in suite.all_configs():
        # Apply perturbation to dataset images
        val_img_dir = Path('datasets/NEU-DET/yolo/images/val')
        backup_dir = Path('/tmp/val_backup')
        perturbed_dir = Path('/tmp/val_perturbed')

        # Backup originals once
        if not backup_dir.exists():
            backup_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
            for img in val_img_dir.glob('*.jpg'):
                import shutil
                shutil.copy2(img, backup_dir / img.name)

        # Apply perturbation
        for img in backup_dir.glob('*.jpg'):
            orig = cv2.imread(str(img))
            degraded = suite.apply(orig, config.name, config.level)
            cv2.imwrite(str(val_img_dir / img.name), degraded)

        # Evaluate on perturbed images
        val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)
        results.append({
            'perturbation': config.name,
            'level': config.level,
            'mAP50': val.box.map50
        })

    # Restore originals
    for img in backup_dir.glob('*.jpg'):
        import shutil
        shutil.copy2(img, val_img_dir / img.name)

    return pd.DataFrame(results)

# Run on both models
baseline_path = f'runs/baseline_neu_det_seed{SEED}/weights/best.pt'
v2_path = f'runs/digisteel_v2_neu_det_seed{SEED}/weights/best.pt'

if Path(baseline_path).exists():
    print('Evaluating baseline robustness...')
    baseline_robust = evaluate_robust(baseline_path, config_path)
    baseline_robust.to_csv('evals/baseline_robustness.csv', index=False)
    print(f'✓ Baseline robustness saved')
else:
    print(f'⚠ Baseline not found: {baseline_path}')
    baseline_robust = None

if Path(v2_path).exists():
    print('Evaluating DigiSteel v2 robustness...')
    v2_robust = evaluate_robust(v2_path, config_path)
    v2_robust.to_csv('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv', index=False)
    print(f'✓ DigiSteel v2 robustness saved')
else:
    print(f'⚠ DigiSteel v2 not found: {v2_path}')
    v2_robust = None
## 7. Comparison Table

    # Per-perturbation breakdown

content.content:
1	"""
2	DigiSteel-YOLO v2 Custom Trainer.
3	
4	Subclasses Ultralytics DetectionTrainer to:
5	1. Register custom modules (GhostConv, WFCA, EMA) into ultralytics namespace
6	2. Inject Inner-WIoU loss into BboxLoss during training
7	3. Enable perturbation-aware training augmentation
8	
9	Usage:
10	    from digisteel.engine.trainer import DigiSteelTrainer, register_custom_modules
11	
12	    register_custom_modules()
13	    trainer = DigiSteelTrainer(overrides={"data": "configs/data/neu_det.yaml", ...})
14	    trainer.train()
15	"""
16	
17	import torch
18	
19	
20	def register_custom_modules():
21	    """
22	    Register custom modules in the Ultralytics namespace.
23	
24	    Must be called BEFORE loading any YAML config that references these modules.
25	    This injects GhostConv, WFCA, and EMA into ultralytics.nn.tasks globals
26	    so the YAML parser can find them by name.
27	    """
28	    import ultralytics.nn.tasks as tasks
29	    from digisteel.modules import EMA, GhostConv, WFCA
30	
31	    tasks.GhostConv = GhostConv
32	    tasks.WFCA = WFCA
33	    tasks.EMA = EMA
34	
35	
36	class InnerWIoUAdapter:
37	    """
38	    Adapter that computes Inner-WIoU IoU values for Ultralytics BboxLoss.
39	
40	    Ultralytics' BboxLoss.forward() computes iou = self.iou(pred_bboxes, target_bboxes)
41	    and feeds the result into the loss. This adapter replaces that iou() call with
42	    our Inner-WIoU computation while keeping the same interface.
43	
44	    The key insight: we don't replace the entire BboxLoss — we just replace the
45	    IoU computation method. This is more robust across Ultralytics versions.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, lambda_weight: float = 0.5):
49	        self.lambda_weight = lambda_weight
50	
51	    def __call__(self, pred_bboxes, target_bboxes, eps=1e-7):
52	        """Compute Inner-WIoU IoU values, matching standard iou() interface."""
53	        from digisteel.modules.inner_wiou import iou as basic_iou
54	
55	        # Standard IoU
56	        iou_val = basic_iou(pred_bboxes, target_bboxes, eps)
57	
58	        # WIoU-style aspect/scale focus
59	        pred_w = pred_bboxes[:, 2] - pred_bboxes[:, 0]
60	        pred_h = pred_bboxes[:, 3] - pred_bboxes[:, 1]
61	        tgt_w = target_bboxes[:, 2] - target_bboxes[:, 0]
62	        tgt_h = target_bboxes[:, 3] - target_bboxes[:, 1]
63	
64	        pw = pred_w.clamp(min=eps)
65	        ph = pred_h.clamp(min=eps)
66	        tw = tgt_w.clamp(min=eps)
67	        th = tgt_h.clamp(min=eps)
68	
69	        aspect_diff = torch.abs(pw / ph - tw / th)
70	        scale_diff = torch.abs(torch.sqrt(pw * ph) - torch.sqrt(tw * th))
71	        focus = torch.exp(-(aspect_diff + scale_diff))
72	
73	        return iou_val * self.lambda_weight + iou_val * focus * (1 - self.lambda_weight)
74	
75	
76	class DigiSteelTrainer:
77	    """
78	    Custom trainer for DigiSteel-YOLO v2.
79	
80	    Wraps Ultralytics DetectionTrainer to inject:
81	    1. Module registration (GhostConv, WFCA, EMA)
82	    2. Inner-WIoU loss patching
83	
84	    Can be used in two ways:
85	    1. As a trainer class (subclasses DetectionTrainer)
86	    2. As a function that patches an existing model
87	
88	    Usage (as function):
89	        from digisteel.engine.trainer import patch_model_for_digisteel
90	        model = YOLO("configs/models/digisteel_v2.yaml")
91	        patch_model_for_digisteel(model)
92	        model.train(...)
93	
94	    Usage (as trainer):
95	        from digisteel.engine.trainer import DigiSteelTrainer
96	        trainer = DigiSteelTrainer(overrides={...})
97	        trainer.train()
98	    """
99	
100	    @staticmethod
101	    def create_trainer(overrides: dict = None):
102	        """
103	        Create an Ultralytics DetectionTrainer with DigiSteel patches applied.
104	
105	        Args:
106	            overrides: Ultralytics trainer overrides dict.
107	
108	        Returns:
109	            Configured DetectionTrainer instance.
110	        """
111	        register_custom_modules()
112	
113	        from ultralytics.models.yolo.detect import DetectionTrainer
114	
115	        trainer = DetectionTrainer(overrides=overrides or {})
116	
117	        # Patch the criterion's bbox_loss IoU computation
118	        _patch_loss_iou(trainer)
119	
120	        return trainer
121	
122	
123	def _patch_loss_iou(trainer):
124	    """
125	    Patch the trainer's loss to use Inner-WIoU.
126	
127	    This replaces the IoU computation inside BboxLoss with our Inner-WIoU
128	    metric. It's a targeted monkey-patch that survives Ultralytics' internal
129	    loss object rebuilds.
130	    """
131	    try:
132	        adapter = InnerWIoUAdapter(lambda_weight=0.5)
133	
134	        # The criterion is built lazily in Ultralytics.
135	        # We hook into the train method to patch after it's built.
136	        original_train = trainer.train
137	
138	        def patched_train(*args, **kwargs):
139	            # Patch after criterion is built
140	            if hasattr(trainer, "model") and hasattr(trainer.model, "criterion"):
141	                criterion = trainer.model.criterion
142	                if hasattr(criterion, "bbox_loss"):
143	                    criterion.bbox_loss.iou = adapter
144	                    print("  [DigiSteel] Patched BboxLoss with Inner-WIoU")
145	            return original_train(*args, **kwargs)
146	
147	        trainer.train = patched_train
148	    except Exception as e:
149	        print(f"  [DigiSteel] Warning: Could not patch loss: {e}")
150	        print("  [DigiSteel] Training will use standard CIoU loss")
151	
152	
153	def patch_model_for_digisteel(model):
154	    """
155	    Patch a YOLO model for DigiSteel training.
156	
157	    Registers custom modules and prepares the model for use with
158	    DigiSteel v2 architecture YAML.
159	
160	    Args:
161	        model: YOLO model instance.
162	
163	    Returns:
164	        The patched model (same instance).
165	    """
166	    register_custom_modules()
167	
168	    if hasattr(model, "model") and model.model is not None:
169	        info = model.model

"""
DigiSteel-YOLO v2 Custom Trainer.

Subclasses Ultralytics DetectionTrainer to:
1. Register custom modules (GhostConv, WFCA, EMA) into ultralytics namespace
2. Inject Inner-WIoU loss into BboxLoss during training
3. Enable perturbation-aware training augmentation

Usage:
    from digisteel.engine.trainer import DigiSteelTrainer, register_custom_modules

    register_custom_modules()
    trainer = DigiSteelTrainer(overrides={"data": "configs/data/neu_det.yaml", ...})
    trainer.train()
"""

import torch


def register_custom_modules():
    """
    Register custom modules in the Ultralytics namespace.

    Must be called BEFORE loading any YAML config that references these modules.
    This injects GhostConv, WFCA, and EMA into ultralytics.nn.tasks globals
    so the YAML parser can find them by name.
    """
    import ultralytics.nn.tasks as tasks
    from digisteel.modules import EMA, GhostConv, WFCA

    tasks.GhostConv = GhostConv
    tasks.WFCA = WFCA
    tasks.EMA = EMA


class InnerWIoUAdapter:
    """
    Adapter that computes Inner-WIoU IoU values for Ultralytics BboxLoss.

    Ultralytics' BboxLoss.forward() computes iou = self.iou(pred_bboxes, target_bboxes)
    and feeds the result into the loss. This adapter replaces that iou() call with
    our Inner-WIoU computation while keeping the same interface.

    The key insight: we don't replace the entire BboxLoss — we just replace the
    IoU computation method. This is more robust across Ultralytics versions.
    """

    def __init__(self, lambda_weight: float = 0.5):
        self.lambda_weight = lambda_weight

    def __call__(self, pred_bboxes, target_bboxes, eps=1e-7):
        """Compute Inner-WIoU IoU values, matching standard iou() interface."""
        from digisteel.modules.inner_wiou import iou as basic_iou

        # Standard IoU
        iou_val = basic_iou(pred_bboxes, target_bboxes, eps)

        # WIoU-style aspect/scale focus
        pred_w = pred_bboxes[:, 2] - pred_bboxes[:, 0]
        pred_h = pred_bboxes[:, 3] - pred_bboxes[:, 1]
        tgt_w = target_bboxes[:, 2] - target_bboxes[:, 0]
        tgt_h = target_bboxes[:, 3] - target_bboxes[:, 1]

        pw = pred_w.clamp(min=eps)
        ph = pred_h.clamp(min=eps)
        tw = tgt_w.clamp(min=eps)
        th = tgt_h.clamp(min=eps)

        aspect_diff = torch.abs(pw / ph - tw / th)
        scale_diff = torch.abs(torch.sqrt(pw * ph) - torch.sqrt(tw * th))
        focus = torch.exp(-(aspect_diff + scale_diff))

        return iou_val * self.lambda_weight + iou_val * focus * (1 - self.lambda_weight)


class DigiSteelTrainer:
    """
    Custom trainer for DigiSteel-YOLO v2.

    Wraps Ultralytics DetectionTrainer to inject:
    1. Module registration (GhostConv, WFCA, EMA)
    2. Inner-WIoU loss patching

    Can be used in two ways:
    1. As a trainer class (subclasses DetectionTrainer)
    2. As a function that patches an existing model

    Usage (as function):
        from digisteel.engine.trainer import patch_model_for_digisteel
        model = YOLO("configs/models/digisteel_v2.yaml")
        patch_model_for_digisteel(model)
        model.train(...)

    Usage (as trainer):
        from digisteel.engine.trainer import DigiSteelTrainer
        trainer = DigiSteelTrainer(overrides={...})
        trainer.train()
    """

    @staticmethod
    def create_trainer(overrides: dict = None):
        """
        Create an Ultralytics DetectionTrainer with DigiSteel patches applied.

        Args:
            overrides: Ultralytics trainer overrides dict.

        Returns:
            Configured DetectionTrainer instance.
        """
        register_custom_modules()

        from ultralytics.models.yolo.detect import DetectionTrainer

        trainer = DetectionTrainer(overrides=overrides or {})

        # Patch the criterion's bbox_loss IoU computation
        _patch_loss_iou(trainer)

        return trainer


def _patch_loss_iou(trainer):
    """
    Patch the trainer's loss to use Inner-WIoU.

    This replaces the IoU computation inside BboxLoss with our Inner-WIoU
    metric. It's a targeted monkey-patch that survives Ultralytics' internal
    loss object rebuilds.
    """
    try:
        adapter = InnerWIoUAdapter(lambda_weight=0.5)

        # The criterion is built lazily in Ultralytics.
        # We hook into the train method to patch after it's built.
        original_train = trainer.train

        def patched_train(*args, **kwargs):
            # Patch after criterion is built
            if hasattr(trainer, "model") and hasattr(trainer.model, "criterion"):
                criterion = trainer.model.criterion
                if hasattr(criterion, "bbox_loss"):
                    criterion.bbox_loss.iou = adapter
                    print("  [DigiSteel] Patched BboxLoss with Inner-WIoU")
            return original_train(*args, **kwargs)

        trainer.train = patched_train
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  [DigiSteel] Warning: Could not patch loss: {e}")
        print("  [DigiSteel] Training will use standard CIoU loss")


def patch_model_for_digisteel(model):
    """
    Patch a YOLO model for DigiSteel training.

    Registers custom modules and prepares the model for use with
    DigiSteel v2 architecture YAML.

    Args:
        model: YOLO model instance.

    Returns:
        The patched model (same instance).
    """
    register_custom_modules()

    if hasattr(model, "model") and model.model is not None:
        info = model.model
        num_params = sum(p.numel() for p in info.parameters()) if hasattr(info, "parameters") else 0
        print(f"  [DigiSteel] Model loaded: {num_params / 1e6:.2f}M params")

        # Check for expected modules
        from digisteel.modules.wfca import WFCA
        from digisteel.modules.ema import EMA
        from digisteel.modules.ghost_conv import GhostConv

        wfca_count = sum(1 for m in info.modules() if isinstance(m, WFCA))
        ema_count = sum(1 for m in info.modules() if isinstance(m, EMA))
        gc_count = sum(1 for m in info.modules() if isinstance(m, GhostConv))

        print(f"  [DigiSteel] Modules: {gc_count} GhostConv, {wfca_count} WFCA, {ema_count} EMA")

    return model

    "#@title 3.3 Prepare Dataset in YOLO Format\n",
    "import random\n",
    "import shutil\n",
    "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "\n",
    "NEU_DET_CLASSES = {\n",
    "    'crazing': 0, 'inclusion': 1, 'patches': 2,\n",
    "    'pitted_surface': 3, 'rolled-in_scale': 4, 'scratches': 5\n",
    "}\n",
    "\n",
    "def convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path, img_w=200, img_h=200):\n",
    "    tree = ET.parse(xml_path)\n",
    "    root = tree.getroot()\n",
    "    lines = []\n",
    "    for obj in root.findall('object'):\n",
    "        name = obj.find('name').text\n",
    "        if name not in NEU_DET_CLASSES:\n",
    "            continue\n",
    "        cid = NEU_DET_CLASSES[name]\n",
    "        bb = obj.find('bndbox')\n",
    "        xmin = float(bb.find('xmin').text)\n",
    "        ymin = float(bb.find('ymin').text)\n",
    "        xmax = float(bb.find('xmax').text)\n",
    "        ymax = float(bb.find('ymax').text)\n",
    "        cx = ((xmin + xmax) / 2) / img_w\n",
    "        cy = ((ymin + ymax) / 2) / img_h\n",
    "        w = (xmax - xmin) / img_w\n",
    "        h = (ymax - ymin) / img_h\n",
    "        lines.append(f'{cid} {cx:.6f} {cy:.6f} {w:.6f} {h:.6f}')\n",
    "    return '\\n'.join(lines)\n",
    "\n",
    "yolo_dir = neu_dir / 'yolo'\n",
    "if yolo_dir.exists() and len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / 'train').glob('*.jpg'))) > 100:\n",
    "    print('✓ YOLO format dataset already exists')\n",
    "    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:\n",
    "        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))\n",
    "        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print('Converting to YOLO format...')\n",
    "    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:\n",
    "        (yolo_dir / 'images' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "    train_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'train_images').glob('*.jpg'))\n",
    "    valid_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'valid_images').glob('*.jpg'))\n",
    "    all_imgs = train_imgs + valid_imgs\n",
    "    ann_dirs = [neu_dir / 'train_annotations'] * len(train_imgs) + [neu_dir / 'valid_annotations'] * len(valid_imgs)\n",
    "\n",
    "    rng = random.Random(42)\n",
    "    converted = 0\n",
    "    for img_path, ann_dir in zip(all_imgs, ann_dirs):\n",
    "        xml_path = ann_dir / (img_path.stem + '.xml')\n",
    "        if not xml_path.exists():\n",
    "            continue\n",
    "        yolo_text = convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path)\n",
    "\n",
    "        r = rng.random()\n",
    "        split = 'train' if r < 0.7 else ('val' if r < 0.9 else 'test')\n",
    "\n",
    "        shutil.copy2(img_path, yolo_dir / 'images' / split / img_path.name)\n",
    "        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split / (img_path.stem + '.txt')).write_text(yolo_text)\n",
    "        converted += 1\n",
    "\n",
    "    print(f'✓ Converted {converted} images')\n",
    "    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:\n",
    "        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))\n",
    "        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')"
   ],
   "execution_count": null,
   "outputs": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. Train Baseline YOLOv11n (pretrained)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "#@title 4.1 Train YOLOv11n Baseline (100 epochs)\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "EPOCHS = 100  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",
    "IMG_SIZE = 640  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",
    "BATCH_SIZE = 16  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",
    "SEED = 42  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",
    "\n",
    "CONFIG_PATH = 'configs/data/neu_det.yaml'\n",
    "BASELINE_NAME = f'baseline_neu_det_seed{SEED}'\n",
    "\n",
    "print('='*60)\n",
    "print('  Training YOLOv11n Baseline (pretrained)')\n",
    "print('='*60)\n",
    "print(f'  Epochs: {EPOCHS}')\n",
    "print(f'  Image: {IMG_SIZE}')\n",
    "print(f'  Batch: {BATCH_SIZE}')\n",
    "print(f'  Init: Pretrained (yolo11n.pt)')\n",
    "print('='*60)\n",
    "\n",
    "baseline_model = YOLO('yolo11n.pt')\n",
    "start = time.time()\n",
    "\n",

    "#@title 4.2 Evaluate Baseline + Measure FPS\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "baseline_path = f'runs/{BASELINE_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(baseline_path).exists():\n",
    "    bmodel = YOLO(baseline_path)\n",
    "    val = bmodel.val(data=CONFIG_PATH, imgsz=IMG_SIZE, batch=BATCH_SIZE, verbose=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # FPS benchmark\n",
    "    import torch\n",
    "    device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'\n",
    "    dummy = torch.randn(1, 3, IMG_SIZE, IMG_SIZE).to(device)\n",
    "    bmodel.model.to(device).eval()\n",
    "    # Warmup\n",
    "    for _ in range(10):\n",
    "        with torch.no_grad():\n",
    "            bmodel.model(dummy)\n",
    "    # Measure\n",
    "    if device == 'cuda':\n",
    "        torch.cuda.synchronize()\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    N = 100\n",
    "    for _ in range(N):\n",
    "        with torch.no_grad():\n",
    "            bmodel.model(dummy)\n",
    "    if device == 'cuda':\n",
    "        torch.cuda.synchronize()\n",
    "    fps = N / (time.time() - t0)\n",
    "\n",
    "    baseline_params = sum(p.numel() for p in bmodel.model.parameters())\n",
    "    print('\\n' + '='*60)\n",
    "    print('  Baseline Results')\n",

    "## 6. Robustness Evaluation"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "#@title 6.1 Run Robustness Sweep (6 types x 4 levels = 24 points)\n",
    "import cv2\n",
    "import shutil\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "from digisteel.perturbations import PerturbationSuite\n",
    "from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules\n",
    "\n",
    "register_custom_modules()\n",
    "\n",
    "def evaluate_robust(model_path, data_yaml, imgsz=640):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Evaluate model on clean + 24 perturbation points.\n",
    "\n",
    "    Uses safe temp directories — original validation images are NEVER modified.\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    model = YOLO(model_path)\n",
    "    suite = PerturbationSuite()\n",
    "    val_img_dir = Path('datasets/NEU-DET/yolo/images/val')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Create a safe working copy of validation images\n",
    "    work_dir = Path('/tmp/val_work')\n",
    "    if work_dir.exists():\n",
    "        shutil.rmtree(work_dir)\n",
    "    work_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n",
    "    for img in val_img_dir.glob('*.jpg'):\n",
    "        shutil.copy2(img, work_dir / img.name)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # We need a temp data yaml that points to our working directory\n",
    "    import yaml\n",
    "    with open(data_yaml) as f:\n",
    "        data_cfg = yaml.safe_load(f)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Clean evaluation (on original images)\n",
    "    clean_val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)\n",
    "    clean_map = clean_val.box.map50\n",
    "    results = [{'perturbation': 'clean', 'level': 0, 'mAP50': clean_map}]\n",
    "    print(f'  Clean: mAP50={clean_map:.4f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Perturbed evaluation\n",
    "    for config in suite.all_configs():\n",
    "        # Apply perturbation to working copy\n",
    "        for img in work_dir.glob('*.jpg'):\n",
    "            orig = cv2.imread(str(img))\n",
    "            if orig is None:\n",
    "                continue\n",
    "            degraded = suite.apply(orig, config.name, config.level)\n",
    "            cv2.imwrite(str(val_img_dir / img.name), degraded)\n",
    "\n",
    "        # Evaluate on perturbed images\n",
    "        val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)\n",
    "        results.append({\n",
    "            'perturbation': config.name,\n",
    "            'level': config.level,\n",
    "            'mAP50': val.box.map50\n",
    "        })\n",
    "        print(f'  {config.name}_L{config.level}: mAP50={val.box.map50:.4f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ALWAYS restore originals from working copy\n",
    "    for img in work_dir.glob('*.jpg'):\n",
    "        shutil.copy2(img, val_img_dir / img.name)\n",
    "    print('  ✓ Original validation images restored')\n",
    "\n",
    "    return pd.DataFrame(results)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Run on both models\n",
    "baseline_weights = f'runs/{BASELINE_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "v2_weights = f'runs/{V2_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(baseline_weights).exists():\n",
    "    print('\\nEvaluating baseline robustness...')\n",
    "    baseline_robust = evaluate_robust(baseline_weights, CONFIG_PATH)\n",
    "    baseline_robust.to_csv('evals/baseline_robustness.csv', index=False)\n",
    "    print('✓ Baseline robustness saved to evals/baseline_robustness.csv')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print(f'⚠ Baseline not found: {baseline_weights}')\n",
    "    baseline_robust = None\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(v2_weights).exists():\n",
    "    print('\\nEvaluating DigiSteel v2 robustness...')\n",
    "    v2_robust = evaluate_robust(v2_weights, CONFIG_PATH)\n",
    "    v2_robust.to_csv('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv', index=False)\n",
    "    print('✓ DigiSteel v2 robustness saved to evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print(f'⚠ DigiSteel v2 not found: {v2_weights}')\n",
    "    v2_robust = None"
   ],
   "execution_count": null,
   "outputs": []
  },

    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "\n",
    "print('='*80)\n",

    "v_metrics_path = Path('evals/v2_metrics.json')\n",

    "if b_metrics_path.exists() and v_metrics_path.exists():\n",
    "    with open(b_metrics_path) as f:\n",

    "    with open(v_metrics_path) as f:\n",
    "        vm = json.load(f)\n",
    "\n",
    "    print(f'\\n{'Metric':<30} {'Baseline':>12} {'DigiSteel v2':>12} {'Delta':>10}')\n",
    "    print('-'*68)\n",
    "    print(f\"{'mAP@0.5':<30} {bm['mAP50']:>12.4f} {vm['mAP50']:>12.4f} {vm['mAP50']-bm['mAP50']:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'mAP@0.5:0.95':<30} {bm['mAP50_95']:>12.4f} {vm['mAP50_95']:>12.4f} {vm['mAP50_95']-bm['mAP50_95']:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'Precision':<30} {bm['precision']:>12.4f} {vm['precision']:>12.4f} {vm['precision']-bm['precision']:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'Recall':<30} {bm['recall']:>12.4f} {vm['recall']:>12.4f} {vm['recall']-bm['recall']:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'Params (M)':<30} {bm['params']/1e6:>12.2f} {vm['params']/1e6:>12.2f} {vm['params']/1e6-bm['params']/1e6:>+10.2f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'FPS':<30} {bm['fps']:>12.1f} {vm['fps']:>12.1f} {vm['fps']-bm['fps']:>+10.1f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Param efficiency\n",
    "    eff_b = bm['mAP50'] / (bm['params'] / 1e6)\n",
    "    eff_v = vm['mAP50'] / (vm['params'] / 1e6)\n",
    "    print(f\"{'Param Efficiency (mAP/M)':<30} {eff_b:>12.4f} {eff_v:>12.4f} {eff_v-eff_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Per-class comparison\n",
    "    classes = list(bm['per_class_ap50'].keys())\n",
    "    print(f'\\n{'Class':<25} {'Baseline':>10} {'v2':>10} {'Delta':>10}')\n",
    "    print('-'*58)\n",
    "    for cls in classes:\n",
    "        b_ap = bm['per_class_ap50'][cls]\n",
    "        v_ap = vm['per_class_ap50'][cls]\n",
    "        print(f\"{cls:<25} {b_ap:>10.4f} {v_ap:>10.4f} {v_ap-b_ap:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Robustness comparison\n",
    "    b_csv = Path('evals/baseline_robustness.csv')\n",
    "    v_csv = Path('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv')\n",
    "    if b_csv.exists() and v_csv.exists():\n",
    "        b_rob = pd.read_csv(b_csv)\n",
    "        v_rob = pd.read_csv(v_csv)\n",
    "\n",
    "        clean_b = b_rob[b_rob['perturbation'] == 'clean']['mAP50'].values[0]\n",
    "        clean_v = v_rob[v_rob['perturbation'] == 'clean']['mAP50'].values[0]\n",
    "        avg_b = b_rob[b_rob['perturbation'] != 'clean']['mAP50'].mean()\n",
    "        avg_v = v_rob[v_rob['perturbation'] != 'clean']['mAP50'].mean()\n",
    "        stab_b = avg_b / clean_b if clean_b > 0 else 0\n",
    "        stab_v = avg_v / clean_v if clean_v > 0 else 0\n",
    "\n",
    "        print(f'\\n{'Robustness Metric':<30} {'Baseline':>12} {'DigiSteel v2':>12} {'Delta':>10}')\n",
    "        print('-'*68)\n",
    "        print(f\"{'Clean mAP@0.5':<30} {clean_b:>12.4f} {clean_v:>12.4f} {clean_v-clean_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "        print(f\"{'Avg Perturbed mAP@0.5':<30} {avg_b:>12.4f} {avg_v:>12.4f} {avg_v-avg_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "        print(f\"{'Stability Ratio':<30} {stab_b:>12.4f} {stab_v:>12.4f} {stab_v-stab_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "        # Per-perturbation breakdown\n",

   ],

  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "#@title 3.3 Prepare Dataset in YOLO Format\n",
    "import random\n",
    "import shutil\n",
    "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "\n",
    "NEU_DET_CLASSES = {\n",
    "    'crazing': 0, 'inclusion': 1, 'patches': 2,\n",
    "    'pitted_surface': 3, 'rolled-in_scale': 4, 'scratches': 5\n",
    "}\n",
    "\n",
    "def convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path, img_w=200, img_h=200):\n",
    "    tree = ET.parse(xml_path)\n",
    "    root = tree.getroot()\n",
    "    lines = []\n",
    "    for obj in root.findall('object'):\n",
    "        name = obj.find('name').text\n",
    "        if name not in NEU_DET_CLASSES:\n",
    "            continue\n",
    "        cid = NEU_DET_CLASSES[name]\n",
    "        bb = obj.find('bndbox')\n",
    "        xmin = float(bb.find('xmin').text)\n",
    "        ymin = float(bb.find('ymin').text)\n",
    "        xmax = float(bb.find('xmax').text)\n",
    "        ymax = float(bb.find('ymax').text)\n",
    "        cx = ((xmin + xmax) / 2) / img_w\n",
    "        cy = ((ymin + ymax) / 2) / img_h\n",
    "        w = (xmax - xmin) / img_w\n",
    "        h = (ymax - ymin) / img_h\n",
    "        lines.append(f'{cid} {cx:.6f} {cy:.6f} {w:.6f} {h:.6f}')\n",
    "    return '\\n'.join(lines)\n",
    "\n",
    "yolo_dir = neu_dir / 'yolo'\n",
    "if yolo_dir.exists() and len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / 'train').glob('*.jpg'))) > 100:\n",
    "    print('✓ YOLO format dataset already exists')\n",
    "    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:\n",
    "        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))\n",
    "        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print('Converting to YOLO format...')\n",
    "    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:\n",
    "        (yolo_dir / 'images' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "    train_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'train_images').glob('*.jpg'))\n",
    "    valid_imgs = list((neu_dir / 'valid_images').glob('*.jpg'))\n",
    "    all_imgs = train_imgs + valid_imgs\n",
    "    ann_dirs = [neu_dir / 'train_annotations'] * len(train_imgs) + [neu_dir / 'valid_annotations'] * len(valid_imgs)\n",
    "\n",
    "    rng = random.Random(42)\n",
    "    converted = 0\n",
    "    for img_path, ann_dir in zip(all_imgs, ann_dirs):\n",
    "        xml_path = ann_dir / (img_path.stem + '.xml')\n",
    "        if not xml_path.exists():\n",
    "            continue\n",
    "        yolo_text = convert_voc_to_yolo(xml_path)\n",
    "\n",
    "        r = rng.random()\n",
    "        split = 'train' if r < 0.7 else ('val' if r < 0.9 else 'test')\n",
    "\n",
    "        shutil.copy2(img_path, yolo_dir / 'images' / split / img_path.name)\n",
    "        (yolo_dir / 'labels' / split / (img_path.stem + '.txt')).write_text(yolo_text)\n",
    "        converted += 1\n",
    "\n",
    "    print(f'✓ Converted {converted} images')\n",
    "    for split in ['train', 'val', 'test']:\n",
    "        n = len(list((yolo_dir / 'images' / split).glob('*.jpg')))\n",
    "        print(f'  {split}: {n} images')"
   ],
   "execution_count": null,
   "outputs": []

    "#@title 4.1 Train YOLOv11n Baseline (100 epochs)\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "EPOCHS = 100  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",
    "IMG_SIZE = 640  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",
    "BATCH_SIZE = 16  #@param {type:\"integer\"}\n",

    "BASELINE_NAME = f'baseline_neu_det_seed{SEED}'\n",
    "\n",
    "print('='*60)\n",
    "print('  Training YOLOv11n Baseline (pretrained)')\n",
    "print('='*60)\n",
    "print(f'  Epochs: {EPOCHS}')\n",
    "print(f'  Image: {IMG_SIZE}')\n",
    "print(f'  Batch: {BATCH_SIZE}')\n",
    "print(f'  Init: Pretrained (yolo11n.pt)')\n",
    "print('='*60)\n",
    "\n",
    "baseline_model = YOLO('yolo11n.pt')\n",
    "start = time.time()\n",
    "\n",
    "baseline_model.train(\n",
    "    data=CONFIG_PATH,\n",
    "    epochs=EPOCHS,\n",

    "\n",

    "print(f'\\n✓ Baseline training complete in {elapsed/3600:.1f} hours')\n",

   ],
   "execution_count": null,
   "outputs": []
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "#@title 4.2 Evaluate Baseline + Measure FPS\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "baseline_path = f'runs/{BASELINE_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(baseline_path).exists():\n",
    "    bmodel = YOLO(baseline_path)\n",
    "    val = bmodel.val(data=CONFIG_PATH, imgsz=IMG_SIZE, batch=BATCH_SIZE, verbose=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # FPS benchmark\n",
    "    import torch\n",
    "    device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'\n",
    "    dummy = torch.randn(1, 3, IMG_SIZE, IMG_SIZE).to(device)\n",
    "    bmodel.model.to(device).eval()\n",
    "    # Warmup\n",
    "    for _ in range(10):\n",
    "        with torch.no_grad():\n",
    "            bmodel.model(dummy)\n",
    "    # Measure\n",
    "    if device == 'cuda':\n",
    "        torch.cuda.synchronize()\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    N = 100\n",
    "    for _ in range(N):\n",
    "        with torch.no_grad():\n",
    "            bmodel.model(dummy)\n",
    "    if device == 'cuda':\n",
    "        torch.cuda.synchronize()\n",
    "    fps = N / (time.time() - t0)\n",
    "\n",
    "    baseline_params = sum(p.numel() for p in bmodel.model.parameters())\n",
    "    print('\\n' + '='*60)\n",
    "    print('  Baseline Results')\n",
    "    print('='*60)\n",
    "    print(f'  mAP@0.5:      {val.box.map50:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  mAP@0.5:0.95: {val.box.map:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  Precision:    {val.box.mp:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  Recall:       {val.box.mr:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  Params:       {baseline_params:,} ({baseline_params/1e6:.2f}M)')\n",
    "    print(f'  FPS:          {fps:.1f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    classes = ['crazing', 'inclusion', 'patches', 'pitted_surface', 'rolled-in_scale', 'scratches']\n",
    "    print('\\n  Per-class mAP@0.5:')\n",
    "    for name, ap in zip(classes, val.box.ap50):\n",
    "        print(f'    {name:<20} {ap:.4f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Save metrics for comparison\n",
    "    import json\n",
    "    Path('evals').mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n",
    "    with open('evals/baseline_metrics.json', 'w') as f:\n",
    "        json.dump({\n",
    "            'model': 'YOLOv11n Baseline',\n",
    "            'mAP50': val.box.map50,\n",
    "            'mAP50_95': val.box.map,\n",
    "            'precision': val.box.mp,\n",
    "            'recall': val.box.mr,\n",
    "            'params': baseline_params,\n",
    "            'fps': fps,\n",
    "            'per_class_ap50': {name: float(ap) for name, ap in zip(classes, val.box.ap50)},\n",
    "        }, f, indent=2)\n",
    "    print('\\n✓ Metrics saved to evals/baseline_metrics.json')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print(f'⚠ Baseline not found: {baseline_path}')\n",
    "    print('  Run training cell first.')"
   ],
   "execution_count": null,

  {

   "source": [
    "#@title 5.1 Train DigiSteel v2 (100 epochs) with Inner-WIoU\n",
    "from digisteel.engine.trainer import DigiSteelTrainer, register_custom_modules\n",

    "#@title 5.2 Evaluate DigiSteel v2 + Measure FPS\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "import time\n",
    "\n",
    "register_custom_modules()\n",
    "\n",
    "v2_path = f'runs/{V2_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(v2_path).exists():\n",
    "    v2model = YOLO(v2_path)\n",
    "    val = v2model.val(data=CONFIG_PATH, imgsz=IMG_SIZE, batch=BATCH_SIZE, verbose=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # FPS benchmark\n",
    "    import torch\n",
    "    device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'\n",
    "    dummy = torch.randn(1, 3, IMG_SIZE, IMG_SIZE).to(device)\n",
    "    v2model.model.to(device).eval()\n",
    "    for _ in range(10):\n",
    "        with torch.no_grad():\n",
    "            v2model.model(dummy)\n",
    "    if device == 'cuda':\n",
    "        torch.cuda.synchronize()\n",
    "    t0 = time.time()\n",
    "    N = 100\n",
    "    for _ in range(N):\n",
    "        with torch.no_grad():\n",
    "            v2model.model(dummy)\n",
    "    if device == 'cuda':\n",
    "        torch.cuda.synchronize()\n",
    "    fps = N / (time.time() - t0)\n",
    "\n",
    "    v2_params = sum(p.numel() for p in v2model.model.parameters())\n",
    "    print('\\n' + '='*60)\n",
    "    print('  DigiSteel v2 Results')\n",
    "    print('='*60)\n",
    "    print(f'  mAP@0.5:      {val.box.map50:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  mAP@0.5:0.95: {val.box.map:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  Precision:    {val.box.mp:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  Recall:       {val.box.mr:.4f}')\n",
    "    print(f'  Params:       {v2_params:,} ({v2_params/1e6:.2f}M)')\n",
    "    print(f'  FPS:          {fps:.1f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    classes = ['crazing', 'inclusion', 'patches', 'pitted_surface', 'rolled-in_scale', 'scratches']\n",
    "    print('\\n  Per-class mAP@0.5:')\n",
    "    for name, ap in zip(classes, val.box.ap50):\n",
    "        print(f'    {name:<20} {ap:.4f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    import json\n",
    "    with open('evals/v2_metrics.json', 'w') as f:\n",
    "        json.dump({\n",
    "            'model': 'DigiSteel v2',\n",
    "            'mAP50': val.box.map50,\n",
    "            'mAP50_95': val.box.map,\n",
    "            'precision': val.box.mp,\n",
    "            'recall': val.box.mr,\n",
    "            'params': v2_params,\n",
    "            'fps': fps,\n",
    "            'per_class_ap50': {name: float(ap) for name, ap in zip(classes, val.box.ap50)},\n",
    "        }, f, indent=2)\n",
    "    print('\\n✓ Metrics saved to evals/v2_metrics.json')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print(f'⚠ DigiSteel v2 not found: {v2_path}')\n",
    "    print('  Run training cell first.')"
   ],

    "## 6. Robustness Evaluation"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "#@title 6.1 Run Robustness Sweep (6 types x 4 levels = 24 points)\n",
    "import cv2\n",
    "import shutil\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "from pathlib import Path\n",
    "from ultralytics import YOLO\n",
    "from digisteel.perturbations import PerturbationSuite\n",
    "from digisteel.engine.trainer import register_custom_modules\n",
    "\n",
    "register_custom_modules()\n",
    "\n",
    "def evaluate_robust(model_path, data_yaml, imgsz=640):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Evaluate model on clean + 24 perturbation points.\n",
    "\n",
    "    Uses safe temp directories — original validation images are NEVER modified.\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    model = YOLO(model_path)\n",
    "    suite = PerturbationSuite()\n",
    "    val_img_dir = Path('datasets/NEU-DET/yolo/images/val')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Create a safe working copy of validation images\n",
    "    work_dir = Path('/tmp/val_work')\n",
    "    if work_dir.exists():\n",
    "        shutil.rmtree(work_dir)\n",
    "    work_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n",
    "    for img in val_img_dir.glob('*.jpg'):\n",
    "        shutil.copy2(img, work_dir / img.name)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # We need a temp data yaml that points to our working directory\n",
    "    import yaml\n",
    "    with open(data_yaml) as f:\n",
    "        data_cfg = yaml.safe_load(f)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Clean evaluation (on original images)\n",
    "    clean_val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)\n",
    "    clean_map = clean_val.box.map50\n",
    "    results = [{'perturbation': 'clean', 'level': 0, 'mAP50': clean_map}]\n",
    "    print(f'  Clean: mAP50={clean_map:.4f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Perturbed evaluation\n",
    "    for config in suite.all_configs():\n",
    "        # Apply perturbation to working copy\n",
    "        for img in work_dir.glob('*.jpg'):\n",
    "            orig = cv2.imread(str(img))\n",
    "            if orig is None:\n",
    "                continue\n",
    "            degraded = suite.apply(orig, config.name, config.level)\n",
    "            cv2.imwrite(str(val_img_dir / img.name), degraded)\n",
    "\n",
    "        # Evaluate on perturbed images\n",
    "        val = model.val(data=data_yaml, imgsz=imgsz, verbose=False)\n",
    "        results.append({\n",
    "            'perturbation': config.name,\n",
    "            'level': config.level,\n",
    "            'mAP50': val.box.map50\n",
    "        })\n",
    "        print(f'  {config.name}_L{config.level}: mAP50={val.box.map50:.4f}')\n",
    "\n",
    "    # ALWAYS restore originals from working copy\n",
    "    for img in work_dir.glob('*.jpg'):\n",
    "        shutil.copy2(img, val_img_dir / img.name)\n",
    "    print('  ✓ Original validation images restored')\n",
    "\n",
    "    return pd.DataFrame(results)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Run on both models\n",
    "baseline_weights = f'runs/{BASELINE_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "v2_weights = f'runs/{V2_NAME}/weights/best.pt'\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(baseline_weights).exists():\n",
    "    print('\\nEvaluating baseline robustness...')\n",
    "    baseline_robust = evaluate_robust(baseline_weights, CONFIG_PATH)\n",
    "    baseline_robust.to_csv('evals/baseline_robustness.csv', index=False)\n",
    "    print('✓ Baseline robustness saved to evals/baseline_robustness.csv')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print(f'⚠ Baseline not found: {baseline_weights}')\n",
    "    baseline_robust = None\n",
    "\n",
    "if Path(v2_weights).exists():\n",
    "    print('\\nEvaluating DigiSteel v2 robustness...')\n",
    "    v2_robust = evaluate_robust(v2_weights, CONFIG_PATH)\n",
    "    v2_robust.to_csv('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv', index=False)\n",
    "    print('✓ DigiSteel v2 robustness saved to evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    print(f'⚠ DigiSteel v2 not found: {v2_weights}')\n",
    "    v2_robust = None"
   ],
   "execution_count": null,
   "outputs": []
  },

    "    print(f\"{'mAP@0.5:0.95':<30} {bm['mAP50_95']:>12.4f} {vm['mAP50_95']:>12.4f} {vm['mAP50_95']-bm['mAP50_95']:>+10.4f}\")\n",

    "    print(f\"{'Recall':<30} {bm['recall']:>12.4f} {vm['recall']:>12.4f} {vm['recall']-bm['recall']:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'Params (M)':<30} {bm['params']/1e6:>12.2f} {vm['params']/1e6:>12.2f} {vm['params']/1e6-bm['params']/1e6:>+10.2f}\")\n",

    "\n",
    "    # Param efficiency\n",

    "\n",
    "    # Per-class comparison\n",
    "    classes = list(bm['per_class_ap50'].keys())\n",
    "    print(f'\\n{'Class':<25} {'Baseline':>10} {'v2':>10} {'Delta':>10}')\n",
    "    print('-'*58)\n",
    "    for cls in classes:\n",
    "        b_ap = bm['per_class_ap50'][cls]\n",
    "        v_ap = vm['per_class_ap50'][cls]\n",
    "        print(f\"{cls:<25} {b_ap:>10.4f} {v_ap:>10.4f} {v_ap-b_ap:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Robustness comparison\n",
    "    b_csv = Path('evals/baseline_robustness.csv')\n",
    "    v_csv = Path('evals/digisteel_v2_robustness.csv')\n",
    "    if b_csv.exists() and v_csv.exists():\n",
    "        b_rob = pd.read_csv(b_csv)\n",
    "        v_rob = pd.read_csv(v_csv)\n",
    "\n",
    "        clean_b = b_rob[b_rob['perturbation'] == 'clean']['mAP50'].values[0]\n",
    "        clean_v = v_rob[v_rob['perturbation'] == 'clean']['mAP50'].values[0]\n",
    "        avg_b = b_rob[b_rob['perturbation'] != 'clean']['mAP50'].mean()\n",
    "        avg_v = v_rob[v_rob['perturbation'] != 'clean']['mAP50'].mean()\n",
    "        stab_b = avg_b / clean_b if clean_b > 0 else 0\n",
    "        stab_v = avg_v / clean_v if clean_v > 0 else 0\n",
    "\n",
    "        print(f'\\n{'Robustness Metric':<30} {'Baseline':>12} {'DigiSteel v2':>12} {'Delta':>10}')\n",
    "        print('-'*68)\n",
    "        print(f\"{'Clean mAP@0.5':<30} {clean_b:>12.4f} {clean_v:>12.4f} {clean_v-clean_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "        print(f\"{'Avg Perturbed mAP@0.5':<30} {avg_b:>12.4f} {avg_v:>12.4f} {avg_v-avg_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "        print(f\"{'Stability Ratio':<30} {stab_b:>12.4f} {stab_v:>12.4f} {stab_v-stab_b:>+10.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "        # Per-perturbation breakdown\n",
    "        print(f'\\n{'Perturbation':<25} {'Level':>6} {'Baseline':>10} {'v2':>10} {'Delta':>10}')\n",
    "        print('-'*65)\n",
